data.title: A wallet suspected of being linked to Bitmine received 50,000 ETH from FalconX, worth $108 million.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, a wallet suspected to be related to Bitmine (0xA17...7dc9) received 50,000 ETH from FalconX, worth $108.37 million.

data.title: UK Government Mandates Use of Domestic Steel in Public Projects
data.description: The UK government has announced a new policy requiring all departments to utilize domestically produced steel for public projects. According to Jin10, this measure aims to support the local steel industry and ensure that public funds are invested in British manufacturing. Departments seeking to procure steel from overseas must provide a valid justification for doing so. This initiative is part of a broader strategy to bolster the national economy and reduce reliance on foreign imports.

data.title: U.S. President Donald Trump: National Guard May Be Deployed to Airports If Necessary
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has announced the potential deployment of the National Guard to airports if deemed necessary. According to Jin10, this measure is being considered to ensure security and manage operations effectively. The decision comes amid heightened concerns over safety and logistical challenges at major airports. Trump emphasized that the deployment would be a precautionary step to maintain order and facilitate smooth functioning. The administration is closely monitoring the situation and will take action based on evolving circumstances.

data.title: The US XRP spot ETF saw a total net inflow of $1.264 million in a single day.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, the XRP spot ETF saw a total net inflow of $1.264 million yesterday (March 25th, Eastern Time). Only the Bitwise XRP ETF (XRP) saw net inflows yesterday, with a single-day net inflow of $1.264 million, bringing its total historical net inflow to $377 million. As of press time, the XRP spot ETF has a total net asset value of $996 million, an XRP net asset ratio of 1.15%, and a historical cumulative net inflow of $1.211 billion.

data.title: U.S. Embassy in Iraq Activates Air Defense Alarm
data.description: The U.S. Embassy in Iraq activated its air defense alarm in the early hours of the 26th local time, according to Jin10. This development highlights ongoing security concerns in the region. Further details on the incident have not been disclosed.

data.title: An account with over $440,000 in profits purchased $300,000 worth of NBA regular season game footage showing the Rockets defeating the Timberwolves.
data.description: According to Odaily Seer, an account (address: 0xb6d6e99d3bfe055874a04279f659f009fd57be17) that profited over $440,000 in the Polymarket "NBA Regular Season Rockets vs. Timberwolves" prediction event purchased $300,000 worth of shares in favor of the Rockets to defeat the Timberwolves, at an opening price of 55.3¢. The NBA regular season game between the Rockets and the Timberwolves will start at 9:30 AM Beijing time today. The NBA regular season is currently nearing its end, with the Rockets holding a 43-28 record, ranking 6th in the Western Conference; the Timberwolves have a 44-28 record, ranking 5th in the Western Conference. Odaily Seer continuously monitors the prediction market, anticipating changes before prices are set.

data.title: Polymarket Introduces Taker Fees Across Most Trading Categories
data.description:
On March 26, Polymarket, a prediction market platform, announced a significant policy change. According to BlockBeats, starting March 30, the platform will implement Taker fees across nearly all trading categories. The new fee structure features variable rates, with cryptocurrency-related contracts reaching a peak rate of 1.8%. Actual fees will vary based on share prices and market dynamics.

Categories such as sports, finance, politics, culture, weather, and general predictions will have lower tiered rates, while mention-based and certain economic predictions will see higher peak rates of around 1.5%.

Notably, geopolitical predictions will remain entirely fee-free, highlighting Polymarket's strategic focus on high-risk global events. This change marks the end of Polymarket's long-standing appeal of offering zero fees.

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | Huaxi Securities Highlights Significant Gold Volatility
data.description:
Gold volatility has significantly increased, according to Huaxi Securities. The implied volatility of gold has risen continuously since last Thursday, reaching 35, which is at the 99.4% historical high percentile since 2009. According to Jin10, this surge is attributed to gold entering a rapid decline phase, awaiting a decrease in volatility.

In the broader context, the long-term support for gold remains intact. On one hand, the accelerated evolution of geopolitical dynamics is marginally weakening the credibility of the U.S. dollar, while the underlying logic of global central banks moving away from dollar dependency remains unchanged. On the other hand, the continuous rise in U.S. debt levels indicates a high reliance on loose monetary policy, suggesting no foundational basis for a trend reversal in gold.

The recent sharp correction in gold prices is largely seen as a deep adjustment following previous excessive gains, with expectations that the subsequent bottoming and recovery process will require a considerable amount of time. A new round of gold market activity may commence once expectations for Federal Reserve interest rate cuts are reignited.

data.title: U.S. President Donald Trump Criticizes Mail-In Voting as Cheating
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has expressed his disdain for mail-in voting, despite having previously won through this method. According to Jin10, Trump stated that he considers mail-in voting to be essentially a form of cheating. His comments come amid ongoing debates about the integrity and security of mail-in ballots in the United States. Trump’s remarks highlight the contentious nature of voting methods and their impact on electoral outcomes.

data.title: Iran Bolsters Defenses on Kharg Island Amid U.S. Military Concerns, Reports Say
data.description: Iran has reportedly intensified its defensive measures on Kharg Island, a key oil export hub, in recent weeks. According to Jin10, citing U.S. media sources, Iran has increased its military presence, including deploying additional air defense units, to counter potential U.S. military actions aimed at seizing the island. Multiple sources indicate that Iran has stationed more shoulder-fired surface-to-air missiles and laid anti-personnel and anti-tank mines along the island's coastline to guard against possible amphibious assaults by U.S. forces.

data.title: Verse8 has raised $5 million in seed funding, co-led by Story Foundation and NEXPACE.
data.description: According to an official announcement, AI-powered game creation platform Verse8 has completed a $5 million seed funding round. The round was co-led by Story Foundation, NEXPACE (Nexon's Web3 division), Neowiz, MARBLEX (Netmarble's Web3 division), Solarium Labs, and NEXUS, with other investors from the gaming, media, and technology sectors. The new funds will be used to advance Verse8's core AI system, expand creator onboarding and incentive programs, and expand its publishing and distribution infrastructure. Founded by JC Kim and Kevin Lee, Verse8 allows creators to generate fully playable multiplayer games in minutes using natural language prompts, without writing code, installing engines, or assembling large production teams. Since its launch in July 2024, Verse8 has accumulated over 3.5 million monthly active users and boasts over 5,000 active creators, generating 25,000 games.

data.title: Foreign Investment in Japanese Stocks Declines by ¥2.51 Trillion
data.description: Foreign investors reduced their holdings in Japanese stocks by ¥2.51 trillion for the week ending March 20, according to Jin10. This marks a significant increase in the outflow compared to the previous week's revised figure of ¥1.77 trillion. The data reflects a growing trend of foreign capital withdrawal from Japan's equity market, raising concerns about investor confidence and market stability.

data.title: Japan's Corporate Service Price Index Rises to 2.7% in February
data.description: Japan's Corporate Service Price Index (CSPI) increased by 2.7% year-on-year in February, according to Jin10. This marks a slight rise from the previous value of 2.6%. The CSPI measures the prices of services provided by businesses to other businesses and is an important indicator of inflationary trends within the corporate sector. The increase suggests a continued upward pressure on service costs, which could impact business expenses and pricing strategies. Analysts are closely monitoring these trends as they may influence broader economic conditions and policy decisions.

data.title: Iranian Media Reports: At Least 10 Rockets Fired from Lebanon Towards Tel Aviv
data.description: According to Tasnim News Agency, at least ten rockets have been launched from Lebanon targeting Tel Aviv. The report highlights escalating tensions in the region, with potential implications for broader geopolitical stability. The situation remains fluid, and further developments are being closely monitored.

data.title: Japanese Investors Brace for Market Volatility Amid Middle East Conflict
data.description:
Investors in Japanese equities are increasingly seeking protection against potential market declines as the conflict in the Middle East continues. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the growing concern among market participants about the impact of geopolitical tensions on global financial markets.

The ongoing conflict has led to heightened uncertainty, prompting investors to adopt defensive strategies to safeguard their portfolios. This trend reflects a broader caution in the market, as geopolitical risks often lead to volatility and unpredictable market movements.

Market analysts suggest that the prolonged nature of the conflict could further influence investor sentiment, potentially affecting stock prices and market stability. As a result, investors are closely monitoring developments in the region, adjusting their strategies accordingly to mitigate potential losses.

The situation underscores the interconnectedness of global markets, where regional conflicts can have far-reaching implications for investors worldwide. As the situation evolves, market participants remain vigilant, ready to respond to any shifts in the geopolitical landscape that could impact their investments.

data.title: Blackstone Reports Low Default Levels in Private Credit Portfolio
data.description: Blackstone's Kenneth Caplan has reported low default levels within the firm's private credit portfolio. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the growing scrutiny in the $1.8 trillion private credit industry. Caplan's observations come amid increasing attention on the sector, which has been experiencing significant fluctuations. The private credit market, valued at $1.8 trillion, is under close examination as industry dynamics continue to evolve.

data.title: South Korea to Submit Supplementary Budget Proposal to Parliament on March 31
data.description: South Korea is set to present a supplementary budget proposal to its National Assembly on March 31. According to Jin10, this move is part of the government's efforts to address economic challenges and stimulate growth. The proposal aims to allocate additional funds to various sectors to bolster the economy amid ongoing global uncertainties. The South Korean government is focusing on enhancing public welfare and supporting industries affected by recent economic disruptions. The supplementary budget is expected to include measures for job creation, support for small and medium-sized enterprises, and investments in infrastructure projects. The proposal will be reviewed by the National Assembly, where it will undergo discussions and potential amendments before approval. This initiative reflects South Korea's commitment to maintaining economic stability and promoting sustainable growth in the face of external pressures.

data.title: UAE Defense Ministry: Responding to Drone and Missile Attacks from Iran
data.description: The United Arab Emirates' Defense Ministry has announced that it is actively responding to drone and missile attacks originating from Iran. According to Jin10, the ministry stated that these attacks have prompted a heightened state of alert and defensive measures to protect national security. The UAE has been coordinating with its allies to address the threat and ensure the safety of its citizens and infrastructure. The situation underscores ongoing regional tensions and the need for diplomatic efforts to prevent further escalation.

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | Spot Silver Drops Over 1% to $70.47 per Ounce
data.description: Spot silver prices have declined by more than 1% today, currently trading at $70.47 per ounce. According to Jin10, this drop reflects ongoing market volatility and investor caution in the precious metals sector. The decline in silver prices comes amid broader market movements and economic uncertainties, influencing investor sentiment and trading strategies.

data.title: Former Goldman Sachs CEO Warns of Potential Asset Overvaluation
data.description: Former Goldman Sachs CEO Lloyd Blankfein has raised concerns about the accumulation of unsold private assets on investors' balance sheets, suggesting that these assets may be overvalued. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting Blankfein's warning that a potential spark could lead to a widespread markdown of these assets. This cautionary note comes amid growing scrutiny of asset valuations in the financial markets. Blankfein's comments underscore the importance of vigilance in assessing the true value of private assets, as market dynamics continue to evolve.
